Who qualifies: Ontario resident with valid health card, long-term (6+ month) physical disability
What it covers: Wheelchairs/mobility & seating, respiratory, oxygen, prosthetics, orthotics, communication aids, enteral feeding, +8,000 items
Coverage: Up to 75% of approved price; 100% for OW/ODSP/ACSD recipients
Vendor requirement: Registered ADP vendor per device category
Onboarding lead time: ~6 months for NEW categories; immediate if already registered
Notes: Core bread-and-butter. Needs registered authorizer (OT/PT/physician) assessment per client.

What it covers: Pays the ADP client co-pay; some supplies/repairs/batteries via Mandatory Special Necessities
Coverage: Covers the 25% ADP gap + select supplies
Notes: Convert every ODSP client to 100%-funded. Pure margin capture on existing pipeline.

Who qualifies: Patients assessed as needing publicly funded home care
What it covers: Equipment & supplies on the provincial formulary
Vendor requirement: Prequalified + on provincial formulary / regional contract (or subcontract to incumbent)
Notes: Incumbents include Medigas/OMS/Rexall. Pursue formulary listing or regional subcontracting.

Who qualifies: Acute patients receiving hospital-level care at home
What it covers: Short-term rentals: beds, oxygen, mobility, monitoring, safety
Coverage: Funded via hospital / Ontario Health atHome
Vendor requirement: Hospital contract or discharge-list vendor; rapid-fulfillment SLA
Notes: $1.1B / 47-site expansion. Rental + fast delivery is the wedge. Build a rental fleet.

What it covers: Assistive devices, mobility, home mods related to the claim
Vendor requirement: Enroll as WSIB provider; per-claim approval
column12: often complex/bariatric. Underused channel for most HME shops.

What it covers: Mobility aids, bathroom safety, CPAP, ostomy/catheter, hearing, prosthetics
Vendor requirement: Register as provider with Medavie Blue Cross

Coverage: 100%; no annual cap when prescribed; direct bill
Notes: Needs Rx + letter of medical necessity + quote + prior approval over standard rate.

Who qualifies: Ontario resident, household income < $60k, disability 1+ yr impeding mobility/ADLs
What it covers: Home mods (ramps, roll-in showers, stairlifts); vehicle mods
Notes: Pairs with mods/install work. Get on installer/vendor radar; help clients apply.

Program: Private insurance (Manulife / Sun Life / Canada Life / GreenShield)
Who qualifies: Plan members with DME benefit + prescription
What it covers: DME: wheelchairs, beds, mobility, CPAP, etc. (medically necessary)
Coverage: Often 100% up to ~$5k-$10k/yr; gap billed to client
Vendor requirement: Assignment of Benefits (AOB) set-up; predetermination per claim
Notes: Biggest fast-unlock. AOB = you bill insurer directly. Verify benefits before quoting.
Stacks with MOD HVMP for home-mod jobs.
Offer financing on co-pays/gaps so price never kills a deal.
